103.reason: [Error: Should not assign TokenType.OR here; this is an invalid single '|', which is a lexical error 'a'. However, setting tokenType to OR will cause it to be treated as a valid token later. Instead, tokenType should remain null or handled separately to ensure error path is taken.]

113.reason: [Error: Same issue as above—assigning TokenType.AND for a single '&' incorrectly treats it as a valid token. This prevents the error from being properly reported because later code may treat it as a valid token instead of triggering the error output path.]

117.reason: [Error: If tokenType was incorrectly set to AND/OR in error cases above, this line won't correct it, and the invalid single '&' or '|' will be output as a valid token instead of triggering error reporting. Additionally, when currentToken is "|" or "&", getType() returns null (since no TokenType has value "|" or "&"), but because tokenType was already set to OR/AND, this check is bypassed.]
